response:
The usual main ingredient of Hákarl is found in the **North Atlantic Ocean and Arctic Ocean**. 

This is stated in the context: "The Greenland shark ("Somniosus microcephalus"), also known as the gurry shark, or grey shark, or by the Kalaallisut name eqalussuaq, is a large shark of the family Somniosidae ("sleeper sharks"), closely related to the Pacific and southern sleeper sharks. The distribution of this species is mostly restricted to the waters of the North Atlantic Ocean and Arctic Ocean."
